title: Workflows
text: This article describes the end-to-end workflows supported by socialinfrascorepy and the server-side processing that happens behind the scenes.

title: Workflows
text:
User read-only workflow
These queries read data that already exists in the database.
import os
import socialinfrascorepy as si

cli = si.client(os.environ["SUPABASE_URL"], os.environ["SUPABASE_ANON_KEY"])
auth = si.sign_in(cli, os.environ["EMAIL"], os.environ["PASSWORD"])
authed = auth["client"]

# Search by place name
candidates = si.search_locations(authed, query="Ithaca", state="NY")

# Retrieve boundary
poly = si.get_boundary_by_osm_id(authed, osm_id=5128581)

# Retrieve sites (up to 1 000; larger sets are randomly sampled)
sites = si.get_sites(authed, location_id="42")

# Retrieve scorecard
scores = si.get_scorecard(authed, osm_id=5128581)

title: Workflows
text:
User request workflow
When a neighborhood is not yet scored, the user submits a request with a valid polygon. The server then:

Validates the geometry (must be Polygon or MultiPolygon, CRS 4326, within the area-size limit).
Uploads the boundary and creates a location entry.
Logs the request in public.requests with status tracking fields: grid, sites, scorecard (all start False), status, and update_time.
Generates a population grid — extracts the regional population raster, crops to the polygon extent, and converts to 1 sq km cells stored in public.neighborhood_grid_cache.
Ingests sites — once the grid is ready (grid = True), the server queries Google Places API using the selected themes and sites_grid_sqkm cell size. When complete, sites is set to True.
Calculates the scorecard — density, diversity, and dispersion metrics are computed, the result row is inserted into public.scorecard_result, and scorecard is set to True.
Sets final status to "success" with the current timestamp.

import time

geom = {
    "type": "Polygon",
    "coordinates": [[
        [-122.32, 47.60], [-122.30, 47.60],
        [-122.30, 47.62], [-122.32, 47.62],
        [-122.32, 47.60],
    ]],
}

result = si.submit_request(
    client=authed,
    geometry=geom,
    theme_ids=[1, 3, 4],
    sites_grid_sqkm=2,
)

# Poll until complete
time.sleep(30)
status = si.get_request_status(authed, result["request"]["id"])
Processing typically takes a few minutes depending on polygon area and theme selection.

title: Workflows
text:
Account management
# Registration and login
si.sign_up(cli, "user@example.com", "password", name="User Name")
auth = si.sign_in(cli, "user@example.com", "password")
authed = auth["client"]

# Password and account lifecycle
si.send_password_reset(authed)
si.delete_account(authed)

# Request history and quotas
si.get_requests(authed)
si.get_subscription(authed)
si.get_usage(authed, start_date="2025-01-01")
si.get_remaining_queries(authed)

Subscription tiers
Every user has a subscription tier. The free tier allows a limited number of queries per month (currently 100 sq km queries). The developer tier (for the core team) has no limit.
Usage is tracked as the product of area (polygon sq km) and n_keywords for each request.

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
socialinfrascorepy lets you use the Social Infrastructure Scorecard from Python. If you use the Social Infrastructure Scorecard Dashboard, this package gives you programmatic access to the same data: view social infrastructure sites (parks, community spaces, places of worship, and more), request scorecards for new areas, and download results for analysis.
You need two environment variables: SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY. These are provided when you sign up for access.

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
socialinfrascorepy lets you use the Social Infrastructure Scorecard from Python. If you use the Social Infrastructure Scorecard Dashboard, this package gives you programmatic access to the same data: view social infrastructure sites (parks, community spaces, places of worship, and more), request scorecards for new areas, and download results for analysis.
You need two environment variables: SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY. These are provided when you sign up for access.

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
Installation
pip install git+https://github.com/timothyfraser/scorecard.git#subdirectory=socialinfrascorepy

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
Step 1 — Create a client
The client() function builds a lightweight configuration object that every other function in the package requires.
import os
import socialinfrascorepy as si

cli = si.client(
    os.environ["SUPABASE_URL"],
    os.environ["SUPABASE_ANON_KEY"],
)

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
Step 2 — Authenticate
Most operations require an authenticated session. Use sign_up() once to register, then sign_in() for subsequent sessions.
# First time only
result = si.sign_up(cli, "you@example.com", "strong-password", name="Your Name")

# Subsequent sessions
auth = si.sign_in(cli, "you@example.com", "strong-password")
authed = auth["client"]
The returned authed client carries your session tokens and is passed to all downstream functions.

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
Step 3 — Look up a neighborhood
You can find neighborhoods by OpenStreetMap ID, internal location ID, area ID, or free-text place name.
# By OSM ID
poly = si.get_boundary_by_osm_id(authed, osm_id=5128581)

# By place name search
candidates = si.search_locations(authed, query="Ithaca", state="NY")
boundary = si.get_boundary_by_place_name(authed, query="Ithaca", state="NY")
Example output (sample):
   location_id   osm_id place_name    county state country  area_km2 geom_type
0        84217  5128581     Ithaca  Tompkins    NY      US     15.41   Polygon

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
Step 4 — Browse themes and keywords
Social infrastructure is organised into themes (e.g. community spaces, parks, places of worship). Browse what is available before submitting a request.
themes = si.get_themes(authed)
keywords = si.get_theme_keywords(authed, theme_ids=[1, 3, 4])

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
Step 5 — Submit a scorecard request
Supply a GeoJSON polygon and, optionally, theme selections. The server validates geometry, checks your quota, and starts asynchronous processing.
import time

geom = {
    "type": "Polygon",
    "coordinates": [[
        [-122.32, 47.60], [-122.30, 47.60],
        [-122.30, 47.62], [-122.32, 47.62],
        [-122.32, 47.60],
    ]],
}

result = si.submit_request(
    client=authed,
    geometry=geom,
    theme_ids=[1, 3, 4],
    sites_grid_sqkm=2,
)

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
section: Step 6 — Poll for completion and retrieve results
text:
Step 6 — Poll for completion and retrieve results
Processing takes a few minutes. Poll the request status and retrieve the scorecard when ready.
time.sleep(30)
status = si.get_request_status(authed, result["request"]["id"])

if status["status"].iloc[0] == "success":
    scores = si.get_scorecard(authed, location_id=result["location_id"])
    sites = si.get_sites(authed, location_id=result["location_id"])

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
Step 7 — Check your account
si.get_subscription(authed)
si.get_usage(authed, start_date="2025-01-01")
si.get_remaining_queries(authed)
si.get_requests(authed)

title: Getting started with socialinfrascorepy
text:
Next steps

Read the Client API article for a complete function reference with parameter details.
See Workflows for an in-depth look at how requests are processed on the backend.

title: Client API reference
text: This article documents every user-facing function in socialinfrascorepy, grouped by capability area. All functions communicate through Supabase Auth and PostgREST. No private API URL is required.

title: Client API reference
text:
Environment requirements



Variable
Required
Purpose




SUPABASE_URL
Always
Supabase project URL


SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
Always
Supabase anon/publishable key



Credentials and safety: The SUPABASE_URL is public. Use only the anon (publishable) key client-side; never expose a service-role or secret key.

title: Client API reference
text:
Client setup
import os
import socialinfrascorepy as si

cli = si.client(
    supabase_url=os.environ["SUPABASE_URL"],
    anon_key=os.environ["SUPABASE_ANON_KEY"],
    access_token=None,
    refresh_token=None,
)
client() returns a lightweight SIClient dataclass that stores connection parameters and (after sign-in) session tokens.

title: Client API reference
text:
Authentication
si.sign_up(client, email, password, name=None)
si.sign_in(client, email, password)
si.send_password_reset(client)
si.delete_account(client)

sign_up() registers a new user; an authenticated client is returned in "client" when the session is created immediately.
sign_in() authenticates and returns "client" (with tokens), "session", and the raw "data".
send_password_reset() requires a signed-in session and triggers a reset email for the authenticated user.
delete_account() calls a server-side RPC that deletes the authenticated user.

title: Client API reference
text:
Themes and keywords
si.get_themes(client)
si.get_theme_keywords(client, theme_ids=None)

get_themes() lists all available social-infrastructure themes.
get_theme_keywords() returns keywords for selected theme IDs (or all themes when None).

title: Client API reference
text:
Polygon lookup
si.search_locations(client, query, country=None, state=None, limit=5)
si.get_boundary_by_osm_id(client, osm_id)
si.get_boundary_by_location_id(client, location_id)
si.get_boundary_by_area_id(client, area_id)
si.get_boundary_by_place_name(client, query, country=None, state=None)
Lookup functions return boundary geometry as a pandas.DataFrame. search_locations() returns candidate rows for fuzzy matching; get_boundary_by_place_name() resolves to a single boundary.

title: Client API reference
text:
Scorecard and sites
si.get_scorecard(client, osm_id=None, location_id=None, limit=100, offset=0)
si.get_sites(client, location_id, limit=1000)

get_scorecard() returns density, diversity, dispersion, and composite scores. Filter by either osm_id or location_id.
get_sites() returns a random sample of social-infrastructure sites within the given location (capped at limit).

title: Client API reference
text:
Request workflow
si.submit_request(
    client,
    geometry,
    n_keywords=None,
    name=None,
    display_name=None,
    place_name=None,
    country="US",
    state=None,
    theme_ids=None,
    sites_grid_sqkm=2,
)

si.get_request_status(client, request_id)
si.get_requests(client, limit=100, offset=0)

Parameters for submit_request()

geometry — GeoJSON geometry dict or JSON string (Polygon / MultiPolygon, CRS 4326).
theme_ids — list of int or CSV string (e.g. [1, 3, 4] or "1,3,4"). Selects keyword groups for site ingestion.
sites_grid_sqkm — cell size for the ephemeral query grid used during Google Places ingestion (default 2 sq km).

title: Client API reference
text:
Account and usage
si.get_subscription(client)
si.get_usage(client, start_date=None, end_date=None)
si.get_remaining_queries(client)

get_subscription() returns the user’s profile including tier.
get_usage() summarises request and query counts in a date range.
get_remaining_queries() returns the remaining monthly quota for the authenticated user.

title: Dashboard (Coming Soon)
text: The Social Infrastructure Scorecard Dashboard is a web interface that will make it easy to explore neighborhood scores, inspect underlying sites, and submit new scorecard requests without writing code.

title: Dashboard (Coming Soon)
text:
What to expect

Interactive map views of social infrastructure by neighborhood
On-demand scorecard lookups and downloadable results
Account-aware usage and request status tracking
Direct compatibility with socialinfrascorepy workflows

title: Dashboard (Coming Soon)
text:
Status
Coming soon.
For early access and updates, follow the main repository: https://github.com/timothyfraser/scorecard

title: socialinfrascorepy
text: socialinfrascorepy lets you use the Social Infrastructure Scorecard from Python. View social infrastructure sites (parks, community spaces, places of worship, and more), request scorecards for new areas, and download results for analysis.

title: socialinfrascorepy
text:
Installation
pip install git+https://github.com/timothyfraser/scorecard.git#subdirectory=socialinfrascorepy

title: socialinfrascorepy
text:
Quick start
import os
import socialinfrascorepy as si

cli = si.client(
    os.environ["SUPABASE_URL"],
    os.environ["SUPABASE_ANON_KEY"],
)

auth = si.sign_in(cli, os.environ["EMAIL"], os.environ["PASSWORD"])
authed = auth["client"]

poly = si.get_boundary_by_osm_id(authed, osm_id=5128581)
scores = si.get_scorecard(authed, osm_id=5128581)
See the Getting Started guide for a complete walkthrough.

title: socialinfrascorepy
text:
Key capabilities







Area
Functions




Auth
sign_up(), sign_in(), send_password_reset(), delete_account()


Boundaries
search_locations(), get_boundary_by_osm_id(), get_boundary_by_place_name(), …


Themes
get_themes(), get_theme_keywords()


Requests
submit_request(), get_request_status(), get_requests()


Scorecard
get_scorecard(), get_sites()


Account
get_subscription(), get_usage(), get_remaining_queries()

title: socialinfrascorepy
text:
Environment variables



Variable
Required
Purpose




SUPABASE_URL
Always
Supabase project URL


SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
Always
Supabase anon/publishable key

title: socialinfrascorepy
text:
Methodology
The querying strategy is based on:

Fraser, T. et al. (2022). Trust but verify: Validating new measures for mapping social infrastructure in cities. Urban Climate, 46, 101287. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.uclim.2022.101287

The scorecard measures three dimensions of social infrastructure: density, diversity, and dispersion, combined into a composite letter-grade score.

title: socialinfrascorepy
text:
Credits
Developed by Tim Fraser and Nabira Ahmad.
For questions, contact Tim Fraser at tmf77@cornell.edu.

title: get_scorecard
text:
get_scorecard(client, osm_id=None, location_id=None, limit=100, offset=0)
Get scorecard results for a location.
Provide exactly one of osm_id or location_id.











Name
Type
Description
Default




client
SIClient
A client from :func:~socialinfrascorepy.client authenticated with :func:~socialinfrascorepy.sign_in.
required


osm_id
int
OSM ID used to resolve a bounds area.
None


location_id
str
Location ID for direct scorecard lookup.
None


limit
int
Maximum rows to return (hard-capped at 100).
100


offset
int
Pagination offset.
0














Name
Type
Description





pandas.DataFrame
Scorecard result rows with density, diversity, dispersion, and composite scores.














Name
Type
Description





SIScorecardError
If neither or both of osm_id / location_id are given, or if offset is negative.






>>> scores = si.get_scorecard(authed, osm_id=5128581)

get_theme_keywords(client, theme_ids=None)
List keywords for given theme IDs.

SIClient
A client from :func:~socialinfrascorepy.client.
required

list of int, str, or None
Integer sequence or comma-separated string of theme IDs. Pass None to retrieve all keywords.
None














Name
Type
Description





pandas.DataFrame
A DataFrame with columns theme, type, and term.






>>> kw = si.get_theme_keywords(cli, theme_ids=[1, 3, 4])

title: Function reference
text:
Create and configure the API client.



client
Create a socialinfrascorepy API client.


SIClient
Lightweight, immutable configuration object used by every API call.






User sign-up, sign-in, password reset, and account deletion.



sign_up
Sign up a new user.


sign_in
Sign in with email and password.


send_password_reset
Request a password-reset email for the signed-in user.


delete_account
Delete the currently authenticated account.






Search and retrieve neighborhood boundaries.



search_locations
Search for locations by place name.


get_boundary_by_osm_id
Get boundary by OpenStreetMap ID.


get_boundary_by_location_id
Get boundary by location ID.


get_boundary_by_area_id
Get boundary by area ID.


get_boundary_by_place_name
Get boundary by place name.






Browse available social-infrastructure themes and keywords.



get_themes
List available social-infrastructure themes.


get_theme_keywords
List keywords for given theme IDs.






Retrieve scorecard results and social-infrastructure sites.



get_scorecard
Get scorecard results for a location.


get_sites
Get social-infrastructure sites for a location.






Submit requests and track processing status.



submit_request
Submit a scorecard request for a new area.


get_request_status
Get status of a submitted request.


get_requests
Get request history for the current user.






Subscription tier, usage summary, and remaining quota.



get_subscription
Get current subscription profile.


get_usage
Get usage summary for a date range.


get_remaining_queries
Get remaining monthly query quota.

title: submit_request
text:
submit_request(
    client,
    geometry,
    n_keywords=None,
    name=None,
    display_name=None,
    place_name=None,
    country='US',
    state=None,
    theme_ids=None,
    sites_grid_sqkm=2,
)
Submit a scorecard request for a new area.
Validates geometry, checks quota, inserts bounds + location + request row, and triggers asynchronous processing – all server-side via fn_submit_request in PostgreSQL.











Name
Type
Description
Default




client
SIClient
A client from :func:~socialinfrascorepy.client authenticated with :func:~socialinfrascorepy.sign_in.
required


geometry
dict or str
GeoJSON geometry (Polygon or MultiPolygon, CRS 4326) as a Python dictionary or a JSON string.
required


n_keywords
int
Number of ingestion keywords. When None and theme_ids is provided, the server derives the count from matching theme keywords.
None


name
str
Short identifier for the polygon.
None


display_name
str
User-facing polygon label.
None


place_name
str
Place name metadata.
None


country
str
Country code or name.
"US"


state
str
State / region metadata.
None


theme_ids
list of int, str, or None
Integer sequence or comma-separated string of theme IDs for keyword selection.
None


sites_grid_sqkm
float
Query-grid cell size in sq km for Google Places ingestion.
2














Name
Type
Description





dict
A dictionary with request, bounds_id, location_id, required_queries, and usage fields.






>>> geom = {
...     "type": "Polygon",
...     "coordinates": [[
...         [-122.32, 47.60], [-122.30, 47.60],
...         [-122.30, 47.62], [-122.32, 47.62],
...         [-122.32, 47.60],
...     ]],
... }
>>> result = si.submit_request(
...     authed, geometry=geom, theme_ids=[1, 3, 4],
... )
